item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, no clue why it's such a low score.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was actually really nice, the idea of being grinded for hours seems specially terrifying but just thinking about being trapped in an extremely narrow and endless corridor also makes me squirm. Well done in the horror deparment at least! Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There was a 2XX that i changed to SCP-2767, but other than that was pretty good.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, the somewhat interesting long exploration (after a very slow build-up of tests, most of which simply repeated the Description) was utterly spoiled by the unconvincing and unnecessary diaries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As sirpudding pointed out, this image is not compatible with the Foundation's license. If you wish to use an image, please make sure it is usable under CC BY-SA.

item.title: Re: STAFF POST: OPEN
item.content:encoded: As per the image policy, all noncompliant images have been edited out. They may not be added back in until compliant sources are found and posted in this thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That appears to be protected by traditional copyright, it even has a copyright notice at the bottom of the page.

item.title: STAFF POST: OPEN
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page. If the images are not wholly your own, please post the image's source as a link (or links) in reply to this post and edit it into your first post in this discussion thread.

e: removed timer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this in the draft forum, and I like it now! Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Solid skip, claustrophobia inducing like 1562

+1

Some spelling/grammatical errors:

Incident report says "Normally, these rooms are stripped of paint after each subsequent test,," extra comma there

Diary portion says "Suppose it was a good idea to live as free as I did up to know."

Is that supposed to be "now"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you!

There'll soon be more to come for this SCP ;)

As the description states, the effect seemingly has no size limit as long as the room is, indeed, a closed space. The issue is that there are only 8 tins of this paint left so it's unlikely such a scale could actually be tested.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ouch. Did not see the diary thing coming. Having recently become a father myself, that hit me right in the gut. I think you definitely made the right call with the short, matter-of-fact entry when the child died.

I like how it almost explains how it was created and what it does, but not really.

+1

Oh, by the way, what would happen if you painted a really large room with it, like a factory production floor or an airplane hangar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normally, these rooms are stripped of paint after each subsequent test, however on ██16/██/15, testing room 8 was re-painted before testing room 7 was stripped.

Normally paint is only ever applied to a single testing room at a time whilst others are stripped after each test. chamber 8 was painted before chamber 7 was stripped. I'll make a small clarification sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't especially understand the events of the Incident Log, or at least what triggered them. Were two layers of SCP-2767 applied to the room by mistake?

Either way, the Recovery Log interested me and the concept is simple but still very intriguing, enough so for +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Competently written, effective communication, and good mix of horror and scientific study.

While I'm not blown away by it, this is solid, and gets my +1.

I would suggest, trying to find something with a bit more hook in the future, as the article was, again, competent but not inspiring.
